title: fedia pallescens subsp. hirsuta
intro: Fedia pallescens subsp. hirsuta, also known as hairy false feverfew, is a plant species that belongs to the family Valerianaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, commonly used for its medicinal properties. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. Fedia pallescens subsp. hirsuta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its medicinal compounds.
